internal-dns.navigation.root_description: Manage DNS resolution for resources that should only be accessible within your private network.
internal-dns.navigation.feedback: Help us improve internal DNS by <0>giving feedback.</0>
internal-dns.common.errors.zone_name: Name must be a root zone (.) or start with a letter, end with a letter or digit, and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens (63 characters or fewer)
internal-dns.onboarding.title: How to use Internal DNS
internal-dns.onboarding.description:
Internal DNS is made up of three parts that work together to resolve DNS queries. You will set your Gateway
 resolver policies to reference your views, which contain your zones.
internal-dns.onboarding.internal_zones_d: Determine what resources are being resolved
internal-dns.onboarding.views_descriptio: Determine groups of zones that can be accessed by Gateway
internal-dns.onboarding.gateway_resolver: Determine which queries get resolved to which internal resources
Under <0>Gateway</0>, create a new resolver policy or edit an existing one
Set your view as the resolver
internal-dns.internal_zones.page_descrip: Define DNS records for internal resources like environment-specific apps, service endpoints, and databases. Zones must be added to a view to be resolvable.
internal-dns.internal_zones.account_quot: You have used <0>%{used} of %{total}</0> available DNS records in this account.
internal-dns.internal_zones.create_descr: This zone name won't be publicly resolvable — make sure it's meaningful to your internal systems. e.g. prod.internal or team1.internal.company.com
internal-dns.internal_zones.create_check: Cloudflare needs DNS records to protect and accelerate your site. You can also do this later.
internal-dns.internal_zones.create_modal: Name must start with a letter, end with a letter or digit, and contain only letters, digits, or hyphens (63 characters or fewer)
internal-dns.internal_zones.table_empty_: No internal zones have been created.
internal-dns.internal_zones.delete_descr: Are you sure you want to delete %{zone}
internal-dns.internal_zones.details_desc: DNS queries for this zone are resolved according to the resolver policy of any view this zone is added to.
internal-dns.internal_zones.settings_des: Manage the details for your internal zone.
internal-dns.internal_zones.settings_lin: Resources in this zone will be available for resolver policies referencing the following views...
internal-dns.internal_zones.settings_ref: Set another zone as a reference for this zone, in case a resolver cannot locate a record.
internal-dns.internal_zones.configure_re: Reference a zone as a back up for this zone. This will help when a request to this zone cannot find a record.
internal-dns.internal_zones.linked_views: Views that contain this zone. Views are referenced by your resolver policies, which control internal DNS resolution.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.form: Add a record manually. You can also import records using our import action above.
This information in this section will not impact DNS record resolution and is only meant for your reference.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.uplo: Drag and drop to upload a BIND file, or <0>select one from your computer</0>
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.empt: Add records to your internal zone
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.dele: Are you sure you want to delete this %{type} record?
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.a_re: A records map a domain name to one or multiple IPv4 address(es).
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.aaaa: AAAA records map a domain name to one or multiple IPv6 address(es).
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.cnam: "Canonical name" (CNAME) records point from an alias domain to a "canonical" domain. CNAME records are used in lieu of an A record, when a domain or subdomain is an alias of another domain.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.mx_r: Mail exchange (MX) records direct emails to a mail exchange server.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.dkim: DomainKeys Identified Mail (DKIM) records ensure email authenticity by cryptographically signing emails.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.spf_: Sender Policy Framework (SPF) records list authorized IP addresses and domains that can send emails on behalf of your domain.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.dmar: Domain-based Message Authentication Reporting and Conformance (DMARC) records determine what happens to an email after it is checked against SPF and DKIM records.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.txt_: Text (TXT) records let you enter text into the DNS system, such as domain ownership verification, email security, or providing general notes or instructions.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.caa_: Certificate Authority Authorization (CAA) records specify which Certificate Authorities (CAs) are allowed to issue certificates for a domain.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.srv_: Service records (SRV) specify a host and port for specific services like voice over IP (VOIP), instant messaging, and more.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.ptr_: DNS pointer records (PTR) are used for reverse DNS lookups.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.soa_: Start of authority (SOA) records store information such as the domain admin email address, when the domain was last updated, and more.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.ns_r: Nameserver (NS) records indicate which server should be used for authoritative DNS.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.uri_: Uniform Resource Identifier records are commonly used as a compliment to NAPTR records and per the RFC, can be used to replace SRV records. As such, they contain a Weight and Priority field as well as Target, similar to SRV.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.cert: The 'certificate record' stores public key certificates.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.dnsk: The 'DNS Key Record' contains a public key used to verify Domain Name System Security Extension (DNSSEC) signatures.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.ds_r: The DS record is used to verify the authenticity of child zones of DNSSEC zones. The DS key record on a parent zone contains a hash of the KSK in a child zone.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.http: HTTPS Service (HTTPS) records provide clients with information about how they should connect to a server upfront, without requiring an initial plaintext HTTP connection.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.loc_: The 'location' record contains geographical information for a domain in the form of longitude and latitude coordinates.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.napt: The 'name authority pointer'record can be combined with an SRV record to dynamically create URI's to point to based on a regular expression.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.sshf: This record stores the "SSH public key fingerprints"; SSH stands for Secure Shell and i's a cryptographic networking protocol for secure communication over an unsecure network.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.tlsa: TLSA records specify which keys are allowed to be used for a given domain when connecting via TLS.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.smim: SMIMEA records function similar to TLSA but are specific to email addresses.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.svcb: Service Binding (SVCB) records allow you to provide a client with information about how it should connect to a server upfront, without the need of an initial plaintext HTTP connection. SVCB is the generalized form of the HTTPS record type.
internal-dns.internal_zones.records.open: OpenPGP Key records store public keys for PGP encryption.
internal-dns.views.title: Views
internal-dns.views.page_create_descripti: Make sure it’s unique and meaningful to your internal organization. e.g. “London office” or “Corp VPN”
internal-dns.views.page_description: Define groups of internal zones to be linked to <0>Gateway resolver policies</0>.
internal-dns.views.page_linked_zones_des: These zones will be resolved according to the resolver policies that reference this view.
internal-dns.views.page_resolver_policie: The zones added to this view will be resolved according to these <0>Gateway resolver policies</0>.
internal-dns.views.delete_description: Are you sure you want to delete %{view}
internal-dns.views.update_modal_descript: Edit the name of your view
internal-dns.views.link_views_descriptio: Choose the internal zones for your view.
internal-dns.views.resolver_policies_too: The Gateway resolver policy that is referencing this view, and the zones within it, to determine how to resolve DNS queries
internal-dns.views.linked_zones_tooltip: The resolver policy referencing this view will resolve queries for these zones.
internal-dns.views.resolver_policies_emp: No resolver policies referencing this view
